description_long: This is a compact, single-story bungalow with a low-pitched, gable roof and a rectangular footprint. The exterior appears to be finished in a muted, olive-green color, with a slightly darker roof. The structure features a simple, functional design with minimal ornamentation, typical of early to mid-20th-century suburban homes. Windows are modestly sized and arranged in a straightforward, practical pattern along the visible facades. The roof overhangs slightly, providing some shade and protection to the windows and entryway. There is no visible porch or veranda, and the overall appearance is utilitarian and unembellished.
description: A small, olive-green, single-story bungalow with a gable roof and simple, practical window arrangement. The design is plain and functional, with no visible porch.
description_view_0: The bungalow is seen from an angle showing the gable roof and a side wall with a small window.
description_view_1: This view shows the short end of the bungalow, highlighting the gable roof and a single window on the facade.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the bungalow is visible, with the gable roof and another small window on the side wall.
description_view_3: The rear or alternate short end of the bungalow is shown, with a plain wall and a single window.
